action_choice_hints: The candidate cell is adjacent to a cell with a specific role that is not yet part of a constructed structure.
The candidate cell is part of a contiguous region of cells sharing a particular role.
The candidate cell is positioned such that selecting it extends an existing structure in a preferred direction.
The candidate cell is not blocked by cells with a restrictive role in the direction of intended expansion.
The candidate cell is closer (in grid steps) to a target role than alternative candidates.
The candidate cell is selected only if a cell with a required role exists somewhere in the grid.
feature_family_hints: Adjacency relations: Needed to determine if the candidate cell is next to cells with specific roles.
Contiguity/grouping: Required to identify whether the candidate cell is part of a connected region of similar-role cells.
Directional extension: Necessary to capture whether the action extends an existing structure along a particular axis.
Blocking/obstruction: Needed to check if the candidate cell is prevented from being chosen due to the presence of restrictive-role cells in certain directions.
Relative proximity: Required to compare distances from the candidate cell to target-role cells versus other candidates.
Existence predicates: Needed to verify if at least one cell with a required role is present in the grid.
